channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump’s proposal for Gaza entangles Arab allies
item.description: President Trump’s proposal to permanently resettle Palestinians from the Gaza Strip is complicating relationships with allies in the Arab world.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Ebola cases in Uganda rise to 9, while 265 others are being monitored under quarantine
item.description: Health authorities in Uganda say Ebola cases in the country have risen to nine while 265 other people are being monitored under quarantine.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Muhumuza, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel's fatal shooting of a pregnant Palestinian woman raises fears in the West Bank
item.description: Palestinians say the fatal shooting of a pregnant 23-year-old in a refugee camp in the northern West Bank signals a worrying trend toward more lethal, warlike tactics by Israel in the occupied territory.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Debre, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine and Russia trade long-range attacks as officials probe possibilities for peace
item.description: Officials say Russian forces have targeted Ukraine’s energy and gas infrastructure in a “massive” nighttime missile attack, while Ukrainian drones struck an important oil refinery inside Russia.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: Hamas brushes off Trump and insists all parties must be committed to ceasefire
item.description: Hamas has brushed off President Donald Trump’s threat that “all hell” will break out if it does not release the remaining hostages held in Gaza by Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Some Israeli soldiers traveling abroad are targeted for alleged war crimes in Gaza
item.description: An Israeli army reservist’s dream vacation in Brazil ended abruptly last month over an accusation that he committed war crimes in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Molly Quell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What Mexico's richest man thinks Trump should do in his second term
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Carlos Slim, Mexico’s richest man and one of the world's wealthiest, addressed a range of topics at his annual conference on Monday.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: At least 55 are dead after a bus plunged into gorge in Guatemala
item.description: GUATEMALA CITY (AP) — At least 55 people are dead after their bus plunged into a gorge and landed under a bridge Monday on the outskirts of Guatemala’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump signs off on new steel and aluminum tariffs
item.description: President Trump on Monday signed proclamations to bolster tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports into the United States and crack down on attempts by China and Russia to evade penalties. Trump in 2018 announced 25 percent tariffs on steel and 10 percent tariffs on aluminum imports.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Russian diplomat says Putin's conditions on Ukraine must be met
item.description: A Russian diplomat said Monday that a Ukraine war peace agreement won't be reached unless Russian President Vladimir Putin’s conditions have been met, according to multiple outlets.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Hamas postpones upcoming hostage release, risking ceasefire collapse
item.description: Hamas announced Monday it will hold back releasing hostages at the end of the week until further notice, according to a statement by the group reported by multiple media outlets. Hamas, designated as a terrorist organization by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Push to eliminate renewable energy goals in Puerto Rico sparks outrage as outages persist
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Puerto Rico legislators on Monday held a contentious public hearing on a governor-backed bill that would eliminate renewable energy goals and extend operations of the island's lone coal-fired plant, long accused of polluting low-income communities.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ecuador's presidential election goes to runoff between conservative incumbent, leftist lawyer
item.description: Ecuador will choose its next president in a runoff election in April between conservative incumbent Daniel Noboa and leftist lawyer Luisa González. Crime is a major issue for voters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: French girl, 11, found dead near school, murder investigation underway
item.description: Investigators in France have launched a murder investigation after a missing 11-year-old girl was found dead in a wooded area near her school.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vatican charity says Trump administration's gutting of USAID is 'reckless' and could kill millions
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — The Vatican's charity said Monday that U.S. plans to gut USAID were “reckless” and could kill millions, while Pope Francis’ point man on development urged the Trump administration to remember Christian principles about caring for others.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump: Palestinians wouldn't have right to return to developed Gaza
item.description: President Trump in a new interview said Palestinians would not have the right to return to the Gaza Strip if it is taken over and developed by the United States, as he has proposed. "No, they wouldn't, because they're going to have much better housing.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Ethnically split Cyprus' rival leaders say they're ready for UN-led meeting to restart peace talks
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — The rival leaders of ethnically divided Cyprus said Monday that they’re ready to take part in a U.N.-led gathering next month that could pave the way to a resumption of formal talks after an eight-year hiatus to resolve one of the world’s most intractable disputes.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump remains committed to US owning Gaza, says Middle East states could help rebuild war-torn area
item.description: President Donald Trump said he is committed to the U.S. purchasing and owning Gaza, but that he may allow Middle Eastern countries to rebuild sections of the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ecuador's conservative incumbent and a leftist lawyer are leading early presidential voting results
item.description: GUAYAQUIL, Ecuador (AP) — Ecuador is likely to choose its next president in a runoff election later this year as early results of a first-round vote Sunday showed a tight difference between conservative President Daniel Noboa and leftist lawyer Luisa González.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: They helped US order airstrikes against Taliban. Now Trump's moves have left those Afghans in limbo
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — They helped the U.S. military order airstrikes against Taliban and Islamic State fighters and worked as drivers and translators during America's longest war. They were set to start new lives in the United States.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, FARNOUSH AMIRI and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dalai Lama's elder brother, who led several rounds of talks with China, dies at 97
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — The elder brother of the Dalai Lama and former chairman of the Tibetan government-in-exile in India, Gyalo Thondup, who led several rounds of talks with China and worked with foreign governments for the Tibetan cause, has died. He was 97.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 7.6 magnitude earthquake shakes the Caribbean, some islands urge residents to leave coastlines
item.description: A magnitude-7.6 earthquake shook the Caribbean Sea south of the Cayman Islands Saturday, according to the U.S. Geological Survey. Several islands and countries urged people near the coastline to move inland but authorities in most places later lifted the tsunami alerts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mother, son die from food poisoning at Dominican Republic resort
item.description: A grieving family filed a lawsuit after a mother and son died from food poisoning while vacationing at a Dominican Republic resort.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: War-torn Lebanon forms its first government in over 2 years to end deadlock
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s new prime minister on Saturday formed the country’s first full-fledged government since 2022.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas frees 3 more hostages as part of ceasefire agreement with Israel
item.description: Hamas released three additional hostages on Saturday in exchange for dozens of Palestinian prisoners as part of the ceasefire deal the terror group reached with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: President Trump says 'we will have relations with North Korea'; it's a 'big asset' that he gets along with Kim
item.description: President Trump met with Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba at the White House and touted his good relationship with Kim Jong Un from meetings in his first term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu: 'No future' in Gaza unless 'Hamas destroyed' amid concern for hostages
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu said Hamas must be 'destroyed,' following discussions with President Trump and lawmakers on the Hill, despite concerns that hard rhetoric could jeopardize hostage releases.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump sanctions on ICC protects US military and government officials, former NSC staffer says
item.description: Amid the International Criminal Court’s issuance of arrest warrants for Israeli leaders for their role in waging a self-defense war against Hamas, President Donald Trump punished the global body, prompting strong reactions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Egypt lobbies against Trump plan to empty Gaza of Palestinians as Israel prepares for it
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel says it has begun preparations for the departure of Palestinians from Gaza despite international rejection of President Donald Trump’s plan to empty the territory of its population.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump’s Gaza Strip proposal reverberates
item.description: Aftershocks reverberated Wednesday from President Trump’s declaration the previous day that the United States should take “long-term ownership” of Gaza and that the Palestinians who live there should be moved.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, WGN 9

item.title: USPS resumes accepting packages from China, Hong Kong
item.description: USPS says it's working with CBP to implement a collection process for new China tariffs to avoid delivery disruptions.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: USPS lifts suspension of Hong Kong and China parcels
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service reversed course Wednesday, saying it would continue to accept all inbound mail and packages from China and Hong Kong.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Saudi Arabia says it won't establish ties with Israel without creation of Palestinian state
item.description: Saudi Arabia said on Wednesday it would not establish ties with Israel without the creation of a Palestinian state.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump warns he left instructions to ‘obliterate’ Iran if he is assassinated
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday said that he has left instructions that if Iran were to assassination him, the country should be destroyed.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation
